The De Beers diamond monopoly controversy revolves around
the company’s control over the global diamond market in the
... early 21st centuries. De Beers, a British company founded in
1888 by Cecil Rhodes, came to dominate the diamond industry
by controlling diamond production and distribution. ... De
Beers’ monopoly was challenged by a South African-based
company, Cut Stone, led by Ernest Oppenheimer, grandson of
..., Cecil Rhodes. Cut Stone accused De Beers of fixing
diamond prices and controlling the market. The controversy
intensified when the South African government, ..., announced
plans to nationalize the diamond industry in 1981. ... In 1998, a
class action lawsuit was filed against De Beers in the United
States by the American Antitrust Institute (AAI)....
